description: An action that declares a variable, declares a raw pointer to it, and prints the dereferenced value. Validated against the real transpiler across 20 variations (different action/variable/pointer names and initial values), all parse/emit/compile/link/run clean.
requires: {var} must be declared via `keep` BEFORE {ptr}'s declaration -- {ptr}'s initializer `&{var}` references it.
{ptr} must be declared via `keep ... as raw pointer to whole number with value &{var}` before it is dereferenced with `*{ptr}`.
common_mistakes: Do not use `set` in place of these `keep` declarations -- `set` requires the variable to already exist, and this pattern's whole body is the declaration, not a later mutation. A real test run, given exactly this construct's plan description, produced `print the text *P` / `set P to &X` / `set X to 42` / `set P to &X` -- never declaring either variable with `keep`, in a nonsensical order, with a redundant final line.
Do not reorder the three lines shown -- each depends on the one declared before it.
example:
action pointer_demo produces nothing
    keep X as whole number with value 42
    keep P as raw pointer to whole number with value &X
    print the text *P and newline
end action
